selection.status_reason: Live thesis monitor refreshed the active weekly pick.

reasons: Price trend remains constructive: 5-day momentum is 10.3% and 20-day momentum is 26.3%.
Trend quality is supported by 70% positive sessions over the last 10 trading days and the price sitting 11.7% relative to its 20-day average.
Risk profile shows daily volatility around 2.4%, downside volatility near 0.8%, and a recent max drawdown of 2.7%.
Volume confirmation is 37.8% versus the trailing baseline, which helps separate steady breakouts from thin momentum.
Relative strength is 8.5% versus SPY over 5 days and 10.7% versus the sector ETF.
News signal is bullish with score 0.86, confidence 0.78, and 0.7 weighted articles across 3 sources.
GPT-5 article classification and company-news provider coverage rated the news flow bullish at 0.86, after adjusting for relevance, recency, source quality, and story concentration.
Evidence quality: 3 articles across 3 sources, 0.7 effective weighted articles, average relevance 0.68, average source quality 0.79, confidence 0.78.
Technical weights were calibrated on 2303 historical symbol-weeks against 5-day excess return (IC -0.03).
Price action and news are pointing in the same direction, which raises trust in the setup.
Overall model score is 0.303 with high confidence.

news_evidence.title: Alphabet’s $460B AI Lock-In (NASDAQ:GOOG)
news_evidence.llm_reason: Direct, company-specific reporting on Q1 Google Cloud revenue, a ~$462B backlog, AI-driven demand/supply dynamics and higher FY26 CapEx. These items are material and could move the stock in the next 1–10 trading days (guidance/backlog and capex reaction).

news_evidence.title: The AI Growth Story Meets Its First Reality Check
news_evidence.llm_reason: Sector-level note that the AI growth story is hitting a reality check. Broad AI sentiment and revisions to growth expectations could pressure Big Tech/AI-exposed stocks like Alphabet over the next several trading days, but the piece is not Alphabet-specific.
